mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the housing market (HOUST) over the past 3 months signals a surge in consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by > 10% over the past 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST growth > 10% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% signals a tightening labor market, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5% over the past 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in industrial production (INDPRO) over the past 2 months signals a surge in supply chain capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases by > 5% over the past 2 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO growth > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in real disposable personal income (RSAFS) over the past 4 months signals a surge in consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases by > 10% over the past 4 months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS growth > 10% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 4.5% over the past 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 7.5% signals a weakening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 7.5% over the past 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 7.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the housing market (HOUST) and a 5% increase in industrial production (INDPRO) over the past 2 months signals a surge in consumer spending and supply chain capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both HOUST and INDPRO growth > 10% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
